title: Eva 333 Blu Ray and DVD sale numbers
display_title: Eva 333 Blu Ray and DVD sale numbers
wikitext:
[]: Eva 3.33 Blu-Ray and DVD sale numbers             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:27 am Accoriding to the weekly Oricon chart here are the numbers for the Blu-Ray and DVD version of Eva 3.33. FIRST WEEK BluRay -312,842 DVD -120,826 Also Eva 3.0 ended its theatrical run with 5,260,000,000 yen (about US$53.7 million) More info here: [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2013-04-30/japan-animation-dvd-ranking-april-22-28[/url] [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2013-04-30/japan-animation-blu-ray-disc-ranking-april-22-28[/url] [url]http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/news/2013-04-29/evangelion-3.0-ends-theatrical-run-with-5.26-billion-yen[/url] EDIT 1 Here are the first week numbers of EVA 2.22 for comparsion. Bluray-357,000 DVD-283,029 EDIT 2 First week numbers of EVA 1.11 Bluray-49.000 DVD-298,000

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:14 am Looks like home video sales dropped slightly. I wonder if that drop is from NGE fans who think not liking New Eva is the way to show it.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:29 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Looks like home video sales dropped slightly. I wonder if that drop is from NGE fans who think not liking New Eva is the way to show it.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:33 am dvds are outdated, especially in a high-tech country like Japan. Most people have a Blu-ray player now. Also... I have no idea about how popular the onlinestreaming stuff is in Japan. In the USA more and more people use hulu and netflix to watch their stuff online instead of buying. Lower sales don't immediatly mean less popularity.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Apr 30, 2013 8:49 am Do you also have the 1.11 sales to compare too? I think 2.22 was the highest overall but I'm not sure. EDIT: @qu4d: I don't think streaming is really big over there. I'm sure if it was, Blu-Ray copies of movies would probably come with Ultraviolet codes.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Tue Apr 30, 2013 10:50 am TheFriskyIan wrote: Do you also have the 1.11 sales to compare too? I think 2.22 was the highest overall but I'm not sure. EDIT: @qu4d: I don't think streaming is really big over there. I'm sure if it was, Blu-Ray copies of movies would probably come with Ultraviolet codes.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Tue Apr 30, 2013 11:17 am qu4d wrote: dvds are outdated, especially in a high-tech country like Japan. Most people have a Blu-ray player now.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Tue Apr 30, 2013 11:35 am Exactly that one!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Apr 30, 2013 4:56 pm So it's confirmed that Ha was the greatest NME film ever, good.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:05 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: So it's confirmed that Ha was the greatest NME film ever, good.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:13 pm Transformer 3 box office: $1,123,746,996 best movie ever!

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:18 pm TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Ha box office take: 4,000,000,000 yen Q box office take: 5,300,000,000 yen

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:30 pm qu4d wrote: Transformer 3 box office: $1,123,746,996 best movie ever!

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:40 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: So it's confirmed that Ha was the greatest NME film ever, good.

[]:             Quote Post by qu4d » Tue Apr 30, 2013 5:41 pm Just wanted to say that the box office is no indicator for a good movie. Transformers 3 is bloody crap and still made a billion dollars. There are many many many awesome movies that don't reach such a box office by far - and they share the same market with Transformers.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:03 pm qu4d wrote: Just wanted to say that the box office is no indicator for a good movie. Transformers 3 is bloody crap and still made a billion dollars. There are many many many awesome movies that don't reach such a box office by far - and they share the same market with Transformers.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:25 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: Oh come on, that's because of the hype after 3 years, those poor souls didn't even know what they were going to see.

[]:             Quote Post by svenge » Tue Apr 30, 2013 6:45 pm The sales numbers for 1.11 aren't directly comparable to 2.22/3.33 since surely many people didn't upgrade from 1.01 to 1.11 (especially if they didn't have a BD player).

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sat May 04, 2013 2:57 pm Monk Ed wrote: You got a problem with Q, Shinchan? But I guess we should take this to another topic and in the Q subforum.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sat May 04, 2013 3:03 pm airman4 wrote: I would not be surprised if some people hate this movie with no reason
